source_jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.


What you’ll be doing

 - Lead the design of end-to-end product experiences, from initial concept through polished execution.

 - Build on our existing design foundations by refining patterns, improving consistency, and contributing to the evolution of our design system.

 - Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and cross functional partners to shape problem definitions, explore solutions, and ship polished outcomes.

 - Translate user insights, data, and feedback into clear interaction models and elegant visual solutions.

 - Produce high quality deliverables including flows, wireframes, prototypes, and final production assets with strong attention to detail.

 - Continuously iterate on shipped work using customer feedback and product metrics to drive measurable improvements.

What we’re looking for

 - 6+ years of professional design experience in a fast-paced environment, with a portfolio that demonstrates your best work.

 - Thrive in an environment where you have autonomy to execute and take initiative, especially in a fast-paced startup.

 - Deep understanding of design systems and a strong sense of visual and interaction design craft.

 - Excellent communication and organizational skills

 - Entrepreneurial spirit, as you'll be joining on the ground floor and building the business

 - Eligible to work in Canada

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.

 - Experience at a fintech.

Perks & Benefits

 - Healthy equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company’s growth.

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond.



Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

This role is for an existing vacancy and we are looking to fill this role immediately.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, SMB
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada. We’re on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players who want to disrupt traditional banking.

As the only digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution for business banking, we’ve seen rapid growth, scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years.

Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and the legacy systems that have been their only option for more than 100 years. Venn gives Canadian entrepreneurs modern financial tools built for how businesses operate today.

We’ve raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors including Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.

 

What You'll Be Doing

 - Own the full sales cycle for a high volume of SMB opportunities across Canada, from first qualified conversation to signed customer.

 - Quickly qualify prospects based on company profile, needs, urgency, decision-making process, and fit.

 - Run clear, focused discovery calls and product demos that show how Venn can help Canadian businesses move faster, simplify banking, and replace outdated financial tools.

 - Manage a large pipeline of active opportunities while maintaining strong follow-up, clear next steps, and consistent deal momentum.

 - Close a high number of smaller, repeatable deals each month through short, efficient sales cycles.

 - Work closely with BDRs to ensure smooth handoffs, fast follow-up, and strong conversion from qualified meetings.

 - Meet or exceed weekly and monthly targets for activity, pipeline, closed-won deals, and revenue.

 - Share feedback on customer objections, competitor insights, market trends, and process improvements to help strengthen Venn’s SMB sales motion.
   
    

What we’re looking for:

 - 1–2 years of quota-carrying SaaS, fintech, or B2B sales experience, ideally in an SMB, transactional, or high-velocity closing role.

 - A proven ability to close a high volume of smaller deals consistently, ideally 25+ deals per month.

 - Experience selling to founders, owners, operators, or decision-makers at small businesses.

 - Strong qualification skills and the ability to quickly identify strong-fit opportunities while disqualifying low-fit prospects early.

 - Comfort managing dozens of active deals at once while staying organized, responsive, and detail-oriented.

 - Experience working in a metrics-driven sales environment with weekly and monthly activity, pipeline, and revenue goals.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain value simply and confidently.

 - CRM discipline and comfort managing pipeline, forecasting, and follow-up in HubSpot.

 - High urgency, resilience, and a bias toward action in a fast-paced startup environment.
   
    

Bonus Points

 - Experience in B2B fintech, banking, payments, spend management, or financial services.

 - Experience working at an early-stage or high-growth startup.
   
    

Perks & Benefits

 - Healthy equity ownership, aligning your success with the company’s growth

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT VENN

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.


What You'll Be Doing

 - Define and manage paid campaign strategy to achieve acquisition targets and reduce customer acquisition costs.

 - Run growth experiments across new or emerging channels to uncover additional scalable acquisition paths.

 - Own the operations, optimization, and execution across all paid channels (Google Ads, Bing Ads, Meta, etc.), delivering against pipeline creation and ROI goals.

 - Test, analyze, and optimize campaigns on a per-channel basis to improve performance and efficiency.

 - Identify and deliver innovation opportunities, bringing fresh creative thinking to maximize paid channel effectiveness.

 - Collaborate with product, partnerships, design, and other cross-functional teams to ensure growth initiatives are seamlessly integrated.

 - Use data and experimentation to iterate on campaigns, reporting learnings and insights that inform strategy.

 - Support go-to-market strategies and execution for new product launches, contributing to messaging and channel mix.



What We’re Looking For

 - 4+ years of experience in performance marketing, growth marketing, or acquisition roles (startup experience preferred).

 - Strong track record managing paid search and social campaigns (Google, Bing, Meta), with proven ability to hit growth, CAC and ROAS goals.

 - Hands-on experience in campaign execution, optimization, and scaling.
   Strong understanding of growth experimentation across multiple channels.

 - Ability to analyze data, run tests, and iterate quickly.

 - Excellent cross-functional collaboration skills, especially with product, partnerships, and design teams.
   
   

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.

 - Experience in fintech or financial services marketing.

 - Experience from a non-traditional background (Eg. 2 years in consulting).



Perks & Benefits

 - Healthy equity ownership.

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond.



Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

This role is for an existing vacancy and we are looking to fill this role immediately.

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Tackle the company's toughest problems in various different areas (Ops, GTM, Partnerships, etc.).

 - Develop and implement innovative solutions to increase the quality of Venn products and deliver positive business outcomes.

 - Work directly with the founders in setting and ensuring operational KPIs are met.

 - Building and optimising scalable processes to continue Venn hyper-growth.

 - Analyze data and provide meaningful insights to internal teams (e.g., Product) working to improve customer experience.
   
   

What You’ll Need

 - 3+ years of work experience in a fast-paced environment in any of the following areas: high growth start-up / management consulting / investment banking.

 - Analytical in nature and a hyper-logical thinker.

 - A solid track record of taking ownership and leading complex projects end to end.

 - A passion for data and experience with tools such as SQL, Python, and Excel to solve problems or improve processes.

 - Natural curiosity, you don’t accept the status quo and can break complex problems into smaller ones.

 - Ability to drive projects and move them from 0 to 1 while collaborating with multiple teams.
   
   

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.

 - Experience at a fintech.

 - Intermediate to advanced knowledge of SQL.
   
   

Perks & Benefits

 - Healthy equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company’s growth.

source_jobs.title: Technical Talent Sourcer
source_jobs.description:
Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth, scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.

This will be a 6 month contract to start with opportunity for extension or conversion into a full time role.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Own end-to-end sourcing across multiple open roles simultaneously, building robust pipelines for technical, go-to-market, and operational positions.

 - Craft creative, personalized outreach that converts passive candidates who may not be familiar with Venn into engaged prospects.

 - Develop and test compelling candidate value propositions that position Venn competitively against remote-first and large-brand competitors.

 - Partner closely with the Talent team and hiring managers to deeply understand role requirements, team gaps, and the ideal candidate profile.

 - Build and maintain talent pipelines for current and future hiring needs, keeping candidate relationships warm.

 - Execute market mapping exercises to identify talent pools, competitor landscapes, and emerging sourcing channels.

 - Track and report on sourcing metrics: outreach response rates, pipeline conversion, time-to-screen, and use that data to refine your approach.
   
   

What You'll Need

 - 2+ years of sourcing or recruiting experience, ideally within a high-growth startup or fast-paced tech environment.

 - A proven track record of sourcing and engaging passive candidates across a variety of roles and functions.

 - Experience using sourcing tools such as Ashby, HeyReach, LinkedIn, Juicebox or similar ATS and CRM platforms.

 - Ability to analyze outreach and pipeline data to iterate on sourcing strategy.

 - Comfortable with low response rates and creative enough to keep testing new angles.
   
   

Nice To Have

 - Experience sourcing for fintech, SaaS, or financial services companies.

 - Experience building sourcing playbooks or contributing to talent brand initiatives.
   
   

Perks & Benefits

 - Competitive salary and equity package.

 - The opportunity to shape how a high-growth fintech scales its team from the ground up.

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits.



Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

This role is for an existing vacancy and we are looking to fill this role immediately.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.


We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 5,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.



We've raised a $26.5m Series A from Tier 1 investors, and we're rapidly growing and looking for a BDR to play a critical role in driving our next phase of growth and changing the future of banking in Canada.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Generate leads and new business opportunities through high-impact outbound outreach.

 - Qualify potential leads and ensure they are a great fit for Venn's products and services.

 - Identify and research target companies to continuously expand our outbound prospect list.

 - Experiment, iterate and improve outbound processes, tooling and messaging.

 - Partner closely with Account Executives and the wider sales team to improve the outbound BDR strategy. You will have a direct impact on helping shape the broader sales motion.

 - Play a foundational role in defining what great looks like for future BDRs at Venn.

What we’re looking for:

 - 1+ years of experience in a BDR, SDR, or software sales role (ideally as an early sales hire or at a fast-growing startup).

 - Strong record of consistently exceeding personal and professional goals.

 - You’re just as comfortable on the phone as in writing, and you can translate business problems into a compelling value proposition.

 - A customer-obsessed mindset, with real curiosity about the businesses you speak to and the challenges they’re trying to solve.

 - A love of learning. Your someone who is always looking for ways to improve your craft, stay current on trends, and bring new ideas to the table.

 - Comfort operating in a fast-moving, changing environment. You stay adaptable, resilient, and energized by building something new.

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.

 - Experience working at or selling into SMB’s.

 - Experience in fintech, banking, or financial services.

Perks & Benefits

 - Healthy equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company’s growth.

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond.



Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

This role is for an existing vacancy and we are looking to fill this role immediately.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is transforming business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we disrupt traditional banking practices. Tired of the big banks’ monopoly, Canadian business owners are seeking innovative alternatives.

We've raised an $21.5m Series A from Tier 1 investors, and we're rapidly growing and looking to hire A-players to change the future of banking in Canada.

 - $21.5M Series A - Betakit Article https://betakit.com/vault-rebrands-to-venn-following-21-5-million-series-a-round/

What You'll Be Doing

 - Oversee Venn's website, content strategy, customer emails, and onboarding flows to improve acquisition and retention.

 - Own and operate all social media channels, including LinkedIn, curating content and case studies for both the Venn brand and our founders to grow awareness and authority

 - Define and refine how Venn's products are perceived in the market, crafting compelling narratives that resonate with different ICPs.

 - Create and execute a strategy for blogs, case studies, email campaigns, and educational content that drive conversions and engagement.

 - Work closely with sales and product to ensure seamless onboarding and communication throughout the lifecycle.

 - Manage and iterate on our website to enhance messaging, drive conversions, and communicate product value effectively.

 - Lead go-to-market strategies and execution for new product launches.

 - Continuously test messaging, content formats, and distribution strategies to refine Venn's market presence.

What we’re looking for:

 - Minimum 3+ years of full-time (non-internship) experience, including a minimum 2+ years at a tier-1 consulting firm, investment bank, or major technology company, plus at least 1 year of experience at a startup, as a founder, or as a creator/influencer

 - Strong understanding of product positioning, content distribution, engagement metrics, and audience growth.

 - Experience optimizing website content, SEO, and conversion rates.

 - Ability to analyze data, run experiments, and iterate based on insights.

 - Excellent copywriting, editing, and storytelling skills.

 - Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, especially with product and sales teams.

 - Ability to manage projects end-to-end, working with internal teams and contractors.

 - Self-starter and able to own and execute strategies end-to-end

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup

 - Experience at a fintech

Perks & Benefits

 - Healthy equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company’s growth

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond

source_jobs.title: Director, Sales
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT VENN

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada. We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks' monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years. We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a high-impact leadership role stepping in to lead our sales organization - you’ll continue to build the sales engine, sharpen the fundamentals, and set the team up to compound. You'll report directly to the co-founders and own the day-to-day of how Venn sells.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Lead, manage and scale a team of Account Executives and BDRs—setting goals, running 1:1s, coaching to quota, and holding the bar on performance.

 - Own sales enablement end-to-end: onboarding, ramping, ongoing training, playbooks, and the tooling that makes the team faster.

 - Run weekly deal review and forecast calls; pressure-test pipeline, unblock late-stage deals, and make sure nothing slips through the cracks.

 - Build the GTM engine for BDRs—sequencing, cadences, territory design, ICP targeting, and the feedback loops between outbound activity and pipeline quality.

 - Partner with the team on overall GTM strategy, pricing experiments, and the metrics that matter (pipeline coverage, conversion rates, ramp time, win rate, ACV).

 - Work cross-functionally with Marketing, Product, and Ops to make sure sales feedback shapes the roadmap and the demand engine.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 7+ years in B2B sales, with 2+ years leading and managing BDRs and/or AEs in a high-growth environment.

 - A track record of hitting (and helping a team hit) quota in a fast-paced startup or scale up.

 - Proven ability to define and execute GTM strategy while building scalable, repeatable motions across segments.

 - Strong sales operating rhythm: forecasting discipline, deal review, pipeline hygiene, and a clear point of view on what great looks like.

 - Coaching instinct. You can listen to a call, watch a demo, or read a deal note and tell someone exactly what to do differently tomorrow.

 - Demonstrated ability to leverage AI to improve sales productivity, pipeline generation, and customer engagement.

 - Experience incorporating AI tools and workflows into prospecting, research, personalization, and deal strategy.




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup

 - Experience selling B2B fintech, SMB financial services, or horizontal SaaS

 - Built or scaled an outbound BDR function from <5 to 10+




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Healthy equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company's growth.

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Data
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Own Venn’s data infrastructure, including pipelines, models, warehouses, and reporting layers that power product, finance, risk, operations, marketing, and leadership decisions.

 - Build reliable ETL and ELT pipelines that ingest, transform, and structure data from our core product, banking partners, card processors, payment systems, accounting integrations, CRM, marketing tools, and internal systems.

 - Design clean, trusted data models and analytics layers that make business-critical data easy to access, understand, and use across the company.

 - Partner with engineering on event schemas, tracking requirements, and measurement architecture to ensure data is reliable, scalable, and built correctly from the start.

 - Improve data quality, observability, documentation, lineage, testing, and governance across the stack.

 - Empower teams across Venn to use AI-assisted workflows, MCPs, and trusted data tools to safely query data, run analysis, and answer business questions independently.

 - Build dashboards, reports, and self-serve workflows for core company metrics, including transaction volume, card spend, FX, balances, revenue, activation, retention, onboarding conversion, acquisition, and risk signals.

 - Support marketing, growth and sales measurement where needed, including attribution, paid media incrementally, web analytics, CRO, lifecycle analytics, SEO, and organic acquisition reporting.

 - Help translate complex data into clear recommendations that influence product, GTM, finance, and leadership decisions.

What You’ll Need

 - 5+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or a highly technical data role.

 - Experience building and maintaining reliable ETL or ELT pipelines using modern data tooling.

 - Experience working with data warehouses, transformation frameworks, BI tools, and event or product analytics systems.

 - Strong understanding of data quality, testing, documentation, lineage, metric definition, and analytics reliability.

 - Strong SQL skills and experience building production-grade data models.

 - Ability to translate ambiguous business questions into clean data models, dashboards, and clear recommendations.

 - Experience partnering with engineering teams on tracking requirements, event schemas, and data infrastructure.

 - Comfort working cross-functionally with product, engineering, finance, operations, growth, marketing, RevOps, and leadership teams.

 - Experience with marketing, growth, or web analytics, including attribution, funnel analysis, paid media measurement, SEO analytics, lifecycle analytics, or CRO.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical data concepts and analytical findings in a way that drives business decisions.

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.

 - Experience at a fintech

Perks & Benefits

 - Competitive salary.

 - Latest tech and equipment.

source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth, scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.



About This Role

This is Venn’s first marketing hire and a foundational role in shaping how the company is positioned, understood, and trusted as we scale.

We are looking for a hands on Head of Marketing to own and build Venn’s marketing from the ground up. This is not a strategy only role. You will be directly responsible for defining Venn’s identity, leading product marketing and launches, creating core narratives, and putting a clear stamp on how Venn shows up in the world.

You will take what Venn already has, including a strong product, rapid growth, and passionate customers, and turn it into a cohesive brand and marketing engine with a clear and differentiated point of view, while partnering closely with Growth, Sales, Product, and Design to ensure consistency and alignment across the organization.

You will operate hands on initially, owning both strategy and execution, while laying the foundation to build and lead a high performing marketing team over time and work very closely with our Head of Growth.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Define and evolve Venn’s identity by establishing a clear, differentiated brand voice and positioning across all customer facing touchpoints.

 - Own product marketing end to end including positioning, messaging, launches, website copy, and sales enablement.

 - Create repeatable narratives and formats that customers recognize and trust across brand, product marketing, and content.

 - Translate complex financial products into simple, credible stories for founders, operators, and partners.

 - Shape how Venn shows up in the market to ensure our story reaches the right audiences through launches, content, and events.

 - Build marketing that turns attention into trust, conviction, and customer action.

 - Own events and field marketing to deepen relationships and establish Venn as a long term platform in Canadian fintech.

 - Set and maintain a high bar for quality, taste, and consistency in close partnership with Design.

 - Partner closely with Growth, Sales, Product, and Design to ensure cohesion and alignment across the organization.
   
   

What You'll Need

 - 8+ years of experience, with at least 3+ years managing a team in high-growth B2B, SaaS, or fintech.

 - A proven track record of positioning and launching complex B2B products clearly and effectively.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills with exceptional editorial judgment and taste.

 - Experience working cross functionally with Product, Design, Sales, and other teams.

 - Comfort operating in fast paced, ambiguous environments with high ownership and accountability.

 - Ability to think strategically while remaining hands on and execution focused.
   
   

Nice To Have

 - Experience in fintech, financial services, or other regulated industries.

 - Experience at an early stage or high growth startup.

 - Experience building marketing foundations or teams from the ground up.
   
   

Perks & Benefits

 - Competitive salary and equity package.

 - Latest tech and equipment.

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits.



Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin,

source_jobs.title: Chief Anti-Money Laundering Officer
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 5,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.

What You'll Be Doing

 - Develop, implement, and monitor internal compliance policies and procedures.

 - Stay up to date with relevant regulations and ensure company-wide adherence.

 - Conduct internal audits and risk assessments.

 - Lead regulatory reporting and liaise with authorities as needed.

 - Train staff on compliance and regulatory best practices.

 - Support due diligence and third-party vendor reviews.

 - Investigate and resolve compliance issues or breaches.

What we’re looking for:

 - 6+ years in compliance, AML, or risk.

 - Strong knowledge of PCMLTFA, RPAA, and other regulatory frameworks.

 - Excellent analytical, communication, and project management skills.

 - Experience in a high-growth fintech is a strong asset.

 - Certified compliance designation (e.g., CAMS) is a plus.

Bonus Points

 - Experience acting as a CAMLO.

 - Knowledge with SQL.

Perks & Benefits

 - Competitive base salary with an uncapped commission structure, allowing you to directly benefit from your success.

 - Large equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company’s growth.

 - Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond.



Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

This role is for an existing vacancy and we are looking to fill this role immediately.

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT VENN

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada. We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks' monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.

We've raised a $26.5m Series A from Tier 1 investors, and we're rapidly growing and looking for a GTM Engineer to build the outbound engine that drives our next phase of growth and changes the future of banking in Canada. You'll start by building the sales engine, and grow into owning the systems that power our full go-to-market motion across sales, marketing, and customer success.




ABOUT THIS ROLE

As Venn enters its next phase of growth, we’re looking for a GTM Engineer to help build the outbound engine that supports our sales team today and our broader go-to-market motion over time.

You’ll work closely with sales and technology teams to develop the systems, data, and workflows that help us identify, prioritize, and engage the right businesses. This role will start with building the infrastructure that supports our sales team.

This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys building practical processes, testing new approaches, and improving how teams work. There isn’t a fully established playbook in place yet, so you’ll have the opportunity to help shape what effective outbound looks like at Venn as we continue scaling across Canada.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Build the email sequences, triggers, and personalization workflows in HubSpot that give our BDR team a repeatable outbound motion.

 - Standard databases fall short for Canadian SMBs — you'll identify unconventional sources (provincial registries, trade associations, public filings, web scraping), build enrichment pipelines, and create a continuously refreshed account list that fuels the phone channel.

 - Design and deploy a signal-based engine that detects buying intent and turns those signals into triggered plays for the BDR team.

 - Build the infrastructure that lets a small team outperform a much larger one: prioritized account queues, auto-generated account briefs, and personalized outreach drafts inside the BDR workflow.

 - Own HubSpot end to end — object model, lifecycle, routing, and reporting.

 - Use AI wherever it makes a rep faster or a play better, and build the frameworks to know when it's actually working.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - You've built outbound motions before at a B2B company including sequences, triggers, personalization workflows and you can point to the pipeline they produced.

 - You've built custom data pipelines. Scraping, cleaning, deduplication, enrichment — and you can do it without an engineering team behind you. When Clay can't solve it, you go get the data yourself.

 - Technical enough to do the work yourself. You can model data, write queries, call APIs, and wire integrations.

 - You think in systems and signals. When something breaks, you fix the root cause. When something works, you figure out why and scale it.

 - You think commercially. You can talk to a BDR about an account as easily as you can talk to a data engineer about a schema.

 - You ship. You'd rather get a v1 in production this week than design the perfect system for three months.

Bonus points:

 - Experience as the first GTM or RevOps hire at an early-stage startup.

 - Familiarity with Canadian business data sources — and the gaps in coverage from standard vendors.

 - Experience selling into or building tooling for SMBs, fintech, or financial services.

 - Built AI agents or LLM-powered workflows in production.

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Own Venn’s data infrastructure, including pipelines, models, warehouses, and reporting layers that power product, finance, risk, operations, marketing, and leadership decisions.

 - Build reliable ETL and ELT pipelines that ingest, transform, and structure data from our core product, banking partners, card processors, payment systems, accounting integrations, CRM, marketing tools, and internal systems.

 - Design clean, trusted data models and analytics layers that make business-critical data easy to access, understand, and use across the company.

 - Partner with engineering on event schemas, tracking requirements, and measurement architecture to ensure data is reliable, scalable, and built correctly from the start.

 - Improve data quality, observability, documentation, lineage, testing, and governance across the stack.

 - Empower teams across Venn to use AI-assisted workflows, MCPs, and trusted data tools to safely query data, run analysis, and answer business questions independently.

 - Build dashboards, reports, and self-serve workflows for core company metrics, including transaction volume, card spend, FX, balances, revenue, activation, retention, onboarding conversion, acquisition, and risk signals.

 - Support marketing, growth and sales measurement where needed, including attribution, paid media incrementally, web analytics, CRO, lifecycle analytics, SEO, and organic acquisition reporting.

 - Help translate complex data into clear recommendations that influence product, GTM, finance, and leadership decisions.

What You’ll Need

 - 5+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or a highly technical data role.

 - Experience building and maintaining reliable ETL or ELT pipelines using modern data tooling.

 - Experience working with data warehouses, transformation frameworks, BI tools, and event or product analytics systems.

 - Strong understanding of data quality, testing, documentation, lineage, metric definition, and analytics reliability.

 - Strong SQL skills and experience building production-grade data models.

 - Ability to translate ambiguous business questions into clean data models, dashboards, and clear recommendations.

 - Experience partnering with engineering teams on tracking requirements, event schemas, and data infrastructure.

 - Comfort working cross-functionally with product, engineering, finance, operations, growth, marketing, RevOps, and leadership teams.

 - Experience with marketing, growth, or web analytics, including attribution, funnel analysis, paid media measurement, SEO analytics, lifecycle analytics, or CRO.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical data concepts and analytical findings in a way that drives business decisions.

Bonus Points

 - Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.

 - Experience at a fintech

Perks & Benefits

 - Competitive salary.

 - Latest tech and equipment.

source_jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Lead
source_jobs.description:
Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth, scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.


We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.

 

The Role

You'll be Venn's sole recruiter. No coordinator, no sourcing team, no hand-holding. You own full-cycle recruiting end-to-end. From drafting JD to offer stage for with a heavy focus on technical roles.



We're a team of 30 and plan to double headcount this year. You'll carry 6 active reqs at a time, working a mix of inbound and outbound pipelines. You'll partner directly with founders and hiring managers to define roles and set the bar.



What You'll Be Doing

 - Own sourcing, screening, scheduling, and closing for all open roles

 - Source and close world-class technical talent

 - Build and manage both inbound pipelines (job posts, employer brand) and outbound sourcing (direct outreach, boolean, AI tools)

 - Define role requirements directly with hiring managers and founders

 - Keep hiring moving without being chased

 - Handle light office management and support new hire onboarding logistics (low time commitment, ~1 hour/week)
   
    

What You'll Need

 - 4+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience: agency, in-house, or both

 - Experience operating independently with minimal oversight, ideally as a sole recruiter or in a small team

 - Proven track record hiring software engineers across different stacks — you don't need to be an engineer, but you need to know enough to source and screen credibly

 - Comfortable carrying 6+ reqs simultaneously without dropping pace or quality

 - AI-native: you use tools to source smarter and move faster, not just as a checkbox

 - Strong communication and organization skills, you keep hiring moving without being chased
   
    

Nice To Have

 - Experience sourcing for fintech, SaaS, or high growth companies

 - Experience with Ashby and Juicebox
   
    

Perks & Benefits

 - Competitive salary and equity package

 - The opportunity to shape how a high-growth fintech scales its team from the ground up

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits

 

Venn is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

This role is for an existing vacancy and we are looking to fill this role immediately.
